published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2018OptimalInference fixes exogenous linear functionals over convex smoothness classes (https://arxiv.org/abs/1511.06028) and KolesarRothe2018DiscreteRD handles fixed discrete RD running variables (https://arxiv.org/abs/1606.04086); neither subsumes the population cost-quality selected cascade cutoff.

Kotte2026UCCI selects isotonic calibrated routing thresholds by constrained cost minimization (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.18796), and Bouchard2026EscalationWorthIt characterizes deterministic cascade frontiers (https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06350); neither gives the same-law non-representability or critical-curvature selected boundary-value object.

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_rdd_manipulation_bounded_v1: sharp RD manipulation bounds from a McCrary envelope and covariate-share caps has adjacent endpoint-kink/binding-constraint geometry, but no selected cascade cutoff or isotonic-score atom target.

published_prior_art.cite: ArmstrongKolesar2020SimpleHonest indexes honest intervals by smoothness budgets (https://arxiv.org/abs/1606.01200), but does not give finite-atom active-set geometry for zero entering a selected cutoff endpoint set.

ArmstrongKolesar2018BandwidthSnooping (https://arxiv.org/abs/1412.0267), BachocPreinerstorferSteinberger2016PostSelection (https://arxiv.org/abs/1611.01043), CalonicoCattaneoFarrell2018OptimalBandwidthRBC (https://arxiv.org/abs/1809.00236), DongKolesar2021RunningVariableMeasurementError (https://arxiv.org/abs/2111.07388), and Pernot2023IsotonicStratification (https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.05180) are adjacent selection, RD, generated-running-variable, or isotonic-score precedents, not a generated-score selected-threshold CI for this cascade target.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:selected-sharp-set / main identification kernel
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The sharp interval is conditional on the proposal-specific range-respecting C^2 curvature-budget extension class.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The selected endpoint interval is a field-tier sharp bound under the stated smooth-extension model, not unrestricted observed-law point identification.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: prop:critical-curvature / M_*(P)
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The sign-certification frontier is accepted as a conditional sensitivity object indexed by the imposed curvature budget M.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Stage 0 should derive monotonicity and sign-certification under the M-indexed extension class, not treat M_*(P) as an externally validated physical threshold.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: conj:postselect-honest-ci / dense inference rung
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The coverage claim is conditional on constructing the data-only generated-score drift envelope hat G_n and selected-threshold critical value in the locally dense regime.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The inference result is an open dense-regime handle for V_cont(P), not a settled theorem for atomic isotonic-score supports.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add a small finite-atom worked example computing L_*(P,M), U_*(P,M), and M_*(P) from primitive score atoms and showing a non-singleton endpoint set.

State the nonemptiness and endpoint-attainment obligations for H_*(P,M) explicitly as lemmas in the Stage 0 route, including how bounded atomic values plus the curvature budget produce a range-respecting representative.
Sharpen Proposition 1 into a named same-law non-representability corollary against Kotte2026UCCI and Bouchard2026EscalationWorthIt, restricted to the non-singleton endpoint regime.

For the inference handle, replace the existential critical-value clause with a named construction route: Gaussian multiplier/snooping process over feasible thresholds plus an orthogonal generated-score drift envelope.

recommended_next_step: Advance to Stage 0 on the field-tier selected-endpoint kernel, carrying the accepted smooth-extension and dense-inference scope caveats forward.
